headline: Spanish Man Returns €150,000 Sorolla Painting Found on Street
meta_description: After discovering an original Sorolla painting abandoned against a wall in Seville, a man from Murcia immediately returned it to its owners.
summary_short: A Spanish man found what he thought was garbage—a painting with a beautiful frame leaning against a garden wall in Seville. It turned out to be a priceless original.
body:
Andrés Hurtado was passing a house in Seville when he spotted a small painting propped against a garden wall. Attracted by its golden frame, he assumed it was destined for the landfill and decided to rescue it.

What Hurtado didn't know was that the painting had been accidentally left behind by a family loading their car for a trip to their beach house. Amid the chaos of honking horns and traffic congestion, they'd simply rushed away and forgotten it.

Using AI technology, Hurtado discovered his find was no ordinary artwork—it was an original by 19th-century Spanish painter Joaquin Sorolla, worth as much as €150,000. An auction house in Madrid confirmed both the authenticity and the staggering value.

But when Hurtado learned that police had received a theft report about a missing Sorolla painting from Seville, he didn't hesitate. "I immediately called the police and told them that the news was not true, that the painting had not been stolen, that I had it," he explained to reporters.

The family, who had been frantically searching over 300 miles away, were overjoyed to be reunited with their artwork. They've since announced they're preparing a reward for the man who chose to do the right thing.
